text: Consider the following React component. What will be logged to the console after 1 second if the button is clicked once?

options.A: The updated count (e.g., 1 if clicked once)
options.B: The count value at the time of the click event (e.g., 0 if clicked once from initial state)
options.C: undefined
options.D: A Promise object

text: You have deeply nested React components requiring a user object. Passing it via props through every component is cumbersome. Which patterns are suitable to avoid 'prop drilling'?

options.A: React Context API
options.B: Redux or Zustand (state management libraries)
options.C: Passing a callback function down to update parent state
options.D: Using `useRef` to directly access child component props

text: Given this Express setup, what will be the output in the console if a GET request hits `/test`?

options.A: Middleware 1, Route Handler, Middleware 2
options.B: Middleware 1, Route Handler
options.C: Route Handler, Middleware 1
options.D: Middleware 2, Route Handler

text: Consider these two Express responses. Which statements correctly describe their behavior or best practices?

options.A: `res.json()` automatically sets the `Content-Type` header to `application/json`.
options.B: `res.send()` can infer the `Content-Type` based on the body type.
options.C: For sending JSON data, `res.send()` is generally preferred for performance.
options.D: `res.json()` is a wrapper around `res.send()` that adds JSON-specific features.

text: Which MongoDB query will find all documents in the 'products' collection where the 'category' is 'Electronics' AND the 'price' is greater than 100?

options.A: { category: 'Electronics', price: { $gt: 100 } }
options.B: { category: 'Electronics' AND price: { $gt: 100 } }
options.C: { category: 'Electronics' || price: { $gt: 100 } }
options.D: { $and: [ { category: 'Electronics' }, { price: { $gt: 100 } } ] }

text: You want a button to be full width on small screens but half width on medium screens and larger. Which Tailwind CSS classes achieve this?

options.A: w-full md:w-1/2
options.B: w-full lg:w-1/2
options.C: sm:w-full md:w-1/2
options.D: w-1/2 md:w-full

text: Consider this React component. What are the potential issues or correct dependency array usages for the `useEffect` hook?

options.A: An empty dependency array `[]` would cause an infinite loop if `userId` changes.
options.B: Omitting the dependency array would fetch data on every render.
options.C: Including `userId` in the dependency array `[userId]` ensures data refetches when `userId` changes.
options.D: Including `setData` in the dependency array `[setData]` is necessary to prevent stale closures.

text: You're using React Context for a theme. If you update the theme value frequently in the provider, what is a common performance pitfall for consumers?

options.A: Only the component directly consuming the context will re-render.
options.B: All components in the provider's subtree will re-render, regardless of whether they use the context value.
options.C: React will automatically memoize context consumers to prevent unnecessary re-renders.
options.D: The context update will only trigger a re-render if the new value is deeply different from the old one.

text: Consider a `Parent` component rendering a `MemoizedChild`. Which actions would cause `MemoizedChild` to re-render?

options.A: Updating the `count` state in `Parent`.
options.B: Updating the `obj` state in `Parent` with a new object reference, e.g., `setObj({ value: 2 })`.
options.C: Passing a new inline function for `onClick` prop on every `Parent` render.
options.D: Wrapping `handleClick` with `useCallback` and `obj` with `useMemo`.

text: Given this Express route, what will happen if `someAsyncOperation()` throws an error?

options.A: The error will be caught by the `catch` block and passed to the error-handling middleware.
options.B: The server will crash if `someAsyncOperation()` throws an unhandled promise rejection.
options.C: The client will receive a 500 status code with 'Something broke!'.
options.D: The `next(error)` call ensures that the error is properly propagated down the middleware chain.

text: You're building an API with Express. Which practices are crucial for implementing a robust authentication middleware?

options.A: Ensure the middleware runs *before* any routes it protects.
options.B: Store sensitive user data (like passwords) directly in `req.user` after authentication.
options.C: Always call `next()` if authentication fails to allow subsequent middleware to handle it.
options.D: Verify token signatures and expiration dates for JWTs.

text: A client sends a POST request with `Content-Type: application/json` and a JSON body. Which Express middleware is needed to access `req.body`?

options.A: `app.use(express.urlencoded({ extended: true }))`
options.B: `app.use(express.json())`
options.C: `app.use(bodyParser.text())`
options.D: No middleware is needed; Express handles JSON bodies by default.

text: Your MongoDB query `db.users.find({ email: 'test@example.com' })` is performing slowly on a large collection. What actions could improve its performance?

options.A: Create an index on the `email` field.
options.B: Increase the server's RAM to cache more data.
options.C: Use a compound index if `email` is often queried with other fields.
options.D: Switch from `find()` to `findOne()` for better performance.

text: Which aggregation pipeline stage in MongoDB is used to filter documents based on specified conditions, similar to the `find()` method?

options.A: $project
options.B: $match
options.C: $lookup
options.D: $sort

text: You need to add a custom color 'brand-blue' and a new font size 'text-xxl' to your Tailwind CSS project. How would you achieve this?

options.A: Add `colors: { 'brand-blue': '#007bff' }` directly under `theme: {}`.
options.B: Add `fontSize: { 'xxl': '2.5rem' }` under `theme: { extend: {} }`.
options.C: Add `colors: { 'brand-blue': '#007bff' }` under `theme: { extend: {} }`.
options.D: Create a new CSS file and define custom classes like `.brand-blue`.

text: Consider this component. If `count` is updated rapidly, what are the implications of the `useEffect` behavior with an empty dependency array?

options.A: The `console.log` will always show 'Current count: 0'.
options.B: The `setCount` call will always increment from 0, leading to `count` stuck at 1.
options.C: This is a classic 'stale closure' problem because `count` is captured from the initial render.
options.D: To fix this, `count` should be added to the dependency array, or `setCount(prevCount => prevCount + 1)` should be used.

text: You are building a complex UI with many interactive elements. What are the benefits of adopting React's Concurrent Features (e.g., `useTransition`, `useDeferredValue`)?

options.A: It allows React to interrupt and resume rendering tasks, improving responsiveness.
options.B: It guarantees that all state updates are synchronous, simplifying debugging.
options.C: It helps prioritize urgent updates (like typing) over less urgent ones (like data fetching).
options.D: It eliminates the need for `React.memo` and `useCallback` for performance optimization.

text: A React component throws an error inside an `onClick` handler. If this component is wrapped in an Error Boundary, what will be the expected behavior?

options.A: The Error Boundary will catch the error and display its fallback UI.
options.B: The error will propagate up the component tree and crash the application.
options.C: React will automatically recover from the error without any visible impact.
options.D: The error will be silently ignored by React.

text: Consider this Express route. What are the implications of `longRunningSyncTask()` on the Node.js server's performance and responsiveness?

options.A: It will block the Node.js event loop, preventing other requests from being processed.
options.B: Only the `/heavy` route will be affected; other routes will remain responsive.
options.C: It can lead to increased latency for all concurrent requests.
options.D: Node.js's asynchronous nature will automatically offload this task to a worker thread.

text: A frontend app on `http://localhost:3000` cannot access an Express API on `http://localhost:5000` due to a CORS error. What are common CORS misconfigurations or solutions?

options.A: The API server might not be sending `Access-Control-Allow-Origin` header.
options.B: The frontend needs to include `withCredentials: true` in its fetch request.
options.C: The API server should configure CORS middleware to allow `http://localhost:3000`.
options.D: The browser is blocking the request due to a server-side error, not CORS.

text: You are building a Node.js API to handle large file uploads (e.g., 1GB). What are the advantages of using streams over buffering the entire file in memory?

options.A: Reduced memory consumption, especially for very large files.
options.B: Faster processing because data is available immediately as it arrives.
options.C: Prevents the Node.js process from crashing due to out-of-memory errors.
options.D: Simplifies error handling as streams abstract away network issues.

text: You need to transfer funds between two user accounts in MongoDB, ensuring atomicity. Which statements about MongoDB transactions are true?

options.A: Transactions ensure that all operations within them either succeed or fail together.
options.B: Transactions are only available for replica sets and sharded clusters, not standalone instances.
options.C: Operations within a transaction are isolated from operations outside the transaction until commit.
options.D: Transactions are always the most performant way to handle related document updates.

text: What is a primary advantage of using Tailwind CSS utility classes compared to writing custom CSS classes for every component?

options.A: It eliminates the need for any CSS knowledge.
options.B: It leads to smaller CSS bundle sizes by default without configuration.
options.C: It encourages consistent design by constraining choices to a predefined system.
options.D: It automatically generates unique class names to prevent naming collisions.

text: Which of the following are valid and idiomatic ways to conditionally render a component or element in React?

options.A: { condition && <Component /> }
options.B: { condition ? <Component /> : null }
options.C: { if (condition) { return <Component />; } }
options.D: { condition || <Component /> }

text: Which statements are true regarding the `_id` field in MongoDB documents?

options.A: Every document in a MongoDB collection must have a unique `_id` field.
options.B: If `_id` is not provided, MongoDB automatically generates an `ObjectId`.
options.C: The `_id` field can be of any data type, including strings, numbers, or even arrays.
options.D: The `_id` field is automatically indexed for faster lookups.
